---
title: "Adding Custom Tables and Figures"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc-depth: 3
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Adding Custom Tables and Figures}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)

library(gt)
```

# Introduction

The main way to create tables and figures for your `asar` report is by using functions within the `stockplotr` package. However, if `stockplotr` doesn't contain the functions to make tables or figures you need, you may wish to add custom tables or figures to your report. Here, we provide multiple workflow options for doing so.

## Choose your workflow

### Comparing workflows

```{r echo=F}
data.frame(
  Option = c(
    "1: Direct image insertion workflow",
    "2: rda-based workflow",
    "3: Direct coding-in-qmd workflow"
  ),
  Summary = c(
    "Take minimal steps to add an external file to your report in this format:\n\n `![Your caption here](your_image.png){width=___in #fig-your_image_label_name}`",
    "Make an rda containing your table/figure, caption, and alternative text, then add each into your report in the same way that non-custom tables/figures are added.",
    "Code, and plot, your table or figure directly in a Quarto document."
  ),
  Appropriate_if = c(
    "- You don't need to package your table/figure, caption, and alternative text in a single file\n\n- Your table/figure is already saved as an external file (e.g., a png)\n\n- Each table/figure is at most 8\" wide, or can be shrunken to 8\" wide and still be legible",
    "- You'd like to create and save your custom tables/figures in a manner that's most consistent with your other tables/figures generated with asar's complementary R package, stockplotr",
    "- You don't need to package your table/figure, caption, and alternative text in a single file\n\n- You don't need to export your table/figure\n\n- Each table/figure is at most 8\" wide, or can be shrunken to 8\" wide and still be legible)"
  ),
  Pros = c(
    "- Faster process than option 2",
    "- More reproducible\n\n- Saves and stores custom tables/figures in a way that mirrors existing tables/figures generated with stockplotr",
    "- Faster process than option 2\n\n- Not necessary to import any files to display a table/figure"
  ),
  Cons = c(
    "- Less reproducible\n\n- Files (i.e., table/figure, caption, and alternative text) aren't saved in a consistent and predictable system\n\n- Captions and alternative text aren't saved with other figures'/tables' texts\n\n- Table text is not machine-readable, creating a barrier for accessibility",
    "- Slower process than options 1 & 3",
    "- Less reproducible\n\n- Files (i.e., table/figure, caption, and alternative text) aren't exported, and therefore not saved in a consistent and predictable system\n\n- Captions and alternative text aren't saved with other figures'/tables' texts"
  )
) |>
  gt() |>
  fmt_markdown(columns = everything()) |>
  tab_header(
    title = md("**Workflow Options for Custom Tables and Figures**")
  ) |>
  cols_label(
    Appropriate_if = "Appropriate if...",
    Option = "Workflow"
  ) |>
  cols_width(
    Option ~ px(140),
    Summary ~ px(220),
    Appropriate_if ~ px(240),
    Pros ~ px(200),
    Cons ~ px(240)
  ) |>
  cols_align(align = "left", columns = everything()) |>
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold", size = px(14)),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) |>
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold", color = "#333333"),
    locations = cells_body(columns = Option)
  ) |>
  opt_row_striping() |>
  tab_options(
    table.width = px(1040),
    data_row.padding = px(10),
    table.border.top.style = "none",
    table_body.hlines.color = "#eeeeee",
    column_labels.border.bottom.color = "#333333",
    column_labels.border.bottom.width = px(2)
  )
```

# Direct image insertion workflow (option 1)

1.  [Write captions and alternative text](#sec-write-caps-alttext)
2.  [Set up Quarto markdown (.qmd) files to display custom tables and figures](#sec-set-up-qmd)
3.  [Update report skeleton](#sec-update-skeleton)

{{< include snippets/write-caps-alttext.Rmd >}}

{{< include snippets/set-up-qmd.Rmd >}}

{{< include snippets/update-skeleton.Rmd >}}

# rda-based workflow (option 2) {#rda-based-workflow-option-2}

1.  [Code your custom tables and figures](#sec-code-custom-figs-tables)
2.  [Write captions and alternative text](#sec-write-caps-alttext)
3.  [Export your custom tables and figures](#sec-export-custom-figs-tables)
4.  [Identify table width, orientation, and if splitting is necessary](#sec-id_width)
5.  [Set up Quarto markdown (.qmd) files to display custom tables and figures](#sec-set-up-qmd)
6.  [Update report skeleton](#sec-update-skeleton)

{{< include snippets/code-custom-figs-tables.Rmd >}}

{{< include snippets/write-caps-alttext.Rmd >}}

{{< include snippets/export-custom-figs-tables.Rmd >}}

{{< include snippets/id_width.Rmd >}}

{{< include snippets/set-up-qmd.Rmd >}}

{{< include snippets/update-skeleton.Rmd >}}

# Direct coding-in-qmd workflow (option 3)

1.  [Code your custom tables and figures](#sec-code-custom-figs-tables)
2.  [Write captions and alternative text](#sec-write-caps-alttext)
3.  [Identify table width, orientation, and if splitting is necessary](#sec-id_width)
4.  [Set up Quarto markdown (.qmd) files to display custom tables and figures](#sec-set-up-qmd)
5.  [Update report skeleton](#sec-update-skeleton)

{{< include snippets/code-custom-figs-tables.Rmd >}}

{{< include snippets/write-caps-alttext.Rmd >}}

{{< include snippets/id_width.Rmd >}}

{{< include snippets/set-up-qmd.Rmd >}}

{{< include snippets/update-skeleton.Rmd >}}
